programmes.show_description: Bready Bready Bready: Jo Brand demonstrates an impressive grasp of street slang, Katy Wix uses a long part of her body to steal something, and David Baddiel takes a sledgehammer to a problem.
Missed the mockers? Have no fear, as Dara O Briain rounds up the wittiest slices of satire from Series 12 of the superb news quiz - with some outrageous outtakes thrown in too.
Compilation: Rob Brydon returns with a special episode of the rib-tickling, fib-tackling comedy panel show, featuring all the best outtakes and unseen bits from series 7.
Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell sort fact from fiction with guests John Cooper Clarke, Judi Love, Chris McCausland and Caroline Quentin.
Messy: Quite complex quizzing. Things get messy as Stephen Fry learns whether Sarah Millican, Eddie Kadi, Noel Fielding and Alan Davies know their mugwumps from their mullipuffs.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Alexander Armstrong is host as comedians Jo Brand and Munya Chawawa join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Preserving: The bikers journey through time, celebrating British food. Here they explore how some of our tastiest treats have evolved from a need to preserve.
Officers intercept an arsenal of weapons being smuggled into Mexico. A consignment of Fentanyl is discovered in a freezer.
Officers discover the fuel tanks on a truck are full of liquid meth. Three men wearing identical clothing cause suspicion. A man is caught smuggling reptiles.
Documentary series. Bernie puts the first-ever Streamliners festival together, but unprecedented levels of flooding in New South Wales threaten to end his dream.
Corsica and Sardinia: Join Rick Stein on a culinary tour of the sun-kissed Mediterranean. In Sardinia, Rick samples an especially good spaghetti with cuttlefish ink and tomato sauce.
A woman is caught trying to enter the US with a baby that isn't hers, a man wanted for a serious crime is intercepted, and a driver has a heart attack while crossing a bridge.
Methamphetamine is found in a shipment of broccoli, an elderly man tries to smuggle pills in protein powder, and a woman receives a humanitarian visa to see her dying son.
Lifeboat Crew: Guy Martin joins the crew of an RNLI lifeboat in his hometown of Grimsby as he tackles a selfless and demanding job where volunteers put their lives on the line every day.
Buckle up for more motoring japes. The team test luxurious cars including the Rolls-Royce Ghost, the Bentley Mulsanne and the Mercedes S65 AMG in Albania, and Jonathan Ross guests.
The motor-mad motormouths discover some of the odd pitfalls of picking up a used car at a low price. Star Trek's Simon Pegg is beamed up to the Reasonably Priced Car.
Preserving: The bikers journey through time, celebrating British food. Here they explore how some of our tastiest treats have evolved from a need to preserve.
Corsica and Sardinia: Join Rick Stein on a culinary tour of the sun-kissed Mediterranean. In Sardinia, Rick samples an especially good spaghetti with cuttlefish ink and tomato sauce.
Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell sort more fact from fiction with guests Angellica Bell, Bob Mortimer, Suggs and Holly Willoughby.
Retired footballer David James, comedians Rhys James and Isy Suttie, and athletics legend Denise Lewis tackle the best-selling author's fun-filled brainteasers in this edition.
More mind-bending puzzles and fun with the ever-genial Pointless personality - he's joined this time by Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams.
Misconceptions: The witty quiz that thrives on fallacies. Sue Perkins, Sara Cox and Chris Addison guest as departing host Stephen Fry docks Alan Davies points one last time.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Martin Lewis is host as comedian Ignacio Lopez and broadcaster Steph McGovern join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
A Cuddle: The competitors are lured to a wasteland for a challenge involving eggs and medical equipment - David Baddiel spray-paints his face and Jo Brand reveals her psychic superpowers.
Dara O Briain spins the Wheel of News once again as Ed Byrne, Milton Jones, Romesh Ranganathan and Katherine Ryan join Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons to rib the big news-makers.
Christmas Special: The jolly panel show goes all Christmassy as Miranda Hart, Stephen Mangan, Barry Cryer and Miles Jupp join the three wise regulars.
Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell sort more fact from fiction with guests Angellica Bell, Bob Mortimer, Suggs and Holly Willoughby.
Misconceptions: The witty quiz that thrives on fallacies. Sue Perkins, Sara Cox and Chris Addison guest as departing host Stephen Fry docks Alan Davies points one last time.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Martin Lewis is host as comedian Ignacio Lopez and broadcaster Steph McGovern join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Cookbooks: The hairy duo cook dishes from their favourite British cookbooks and tell the story of iconic food writers, including Elizabeth David and Isabella Beeton.
Host Rob Brydon joins David Mitchell and Lee Mack - plus guests Fern Britton, Richard E Grant and Sanjeev Bhaskar - for the panel show that tries to discern fact from fiction.
Rob Brydon hosts the jocular panel show about truth-bending and lie-telling, with David Mitchell and Lee Mack and guests Jason Manford, Ruth Jones, Jack Dee and Peter Serafinowicz.
Rob Brydon hosts the devious and deceitful comedy panel show. Regulars David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome guests Stephen Mangan, Keeley Hawes, Kevin Bridges and Prof Brian Cox.
Rob Brydon hosts the show in which celebs tell tall tales - and a few true stories - about themselves. With guests Ben Fogle, Craig Revel Horwood, Kate Silverton and Hugh Dennis.
Rob Brydon hosts as more celebrities tell tall tales. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack here are John Bishop, Joanna Page, Chris Addison and Patsy Palmer.
Rob Brydon hosts the Machiavellian panel show. Captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack and guests Rhod Gilbert, Miranda Hart and Rufus Hound attempt to out-bluff their opposition.
Rob Brydon presides over the wittily deceptive comedy panel show. David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Patrick Kielty, Bernard Cribbins, Deborah Meaden and Mark Watson.
Rob Brydon chairs the deceptive comedy panel game. David Mitchell and Lee Mack bluff and banter with Sarah Millican, Holly Walsh, Julian Clary and the late Ronnie Corbett.
The Unseen Bits: The gags that didn't make it to TV first time round but will still make you giggle - Rob Brydon hosts the panel show in which fact and fiction are indistinguishable.
Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show featuring David Mitchell and Lee Mack. Rebecca Front and Jack Whitehall challenge Nick Hewer and Miranda Hart to tell fact from fiction.
The guests sorting the fact from fiction with Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell this time are Sophie Ellis-Bextor, Loyiso Gola, Jason Manford and Briony May Williams.
Rob Brydon is back in the host's chair as Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by Pam Ayres, Sarah Kendall, Richard Osman and Kiell Smith-Bynoe this time.
Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are sorting fact from fiction with Lady Leshurr, Philippa Perry, Alan Titchmarsh and Mike Wozniak this time.
The stellar guests joining host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell this time are Victoria Derbyshire, Rhod Gilbert, Rosie Jones and Jamali Maddix.
The celebrity guests joining Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell for this edition are Raj Bisram, Deborah Frances-White, Stephen Mulhern and Jenny Ryan.
Joining Rob Brydon and lightning-quick team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell to sort fact from fiction this time are Jo Brand, Sam Quek, Bobby Seagull and Joe Thomas.
Rob Brydon welcomes more wily guests to the deceptive comedy panel show. Joining captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are Robert Webb, Sir Terry Wogan, Katy Wix and Kevin Bridges.
Tall tales from celebrity panellists. Host Rob Brydon and regulars David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome guests David O'Doherty, Katherine Parkinson, Bill Turnbull and Louie Spence.
Challenging each other to discern fact from fiction here are Greg Davies, Konnie Huq, Phil Tufnell and Marcus Brigstocke. With regulars Rob Brydon, David Mitchell and Lee Mack.
Mix and Match: Mind-improving miscellany with mine of information Stephen Fry, who's also fond of a magic trick. With Jo Brand, James Acaster, Bill Bailey and Alan Davies.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Martin Clunes is host as journalist Lyse Doucet and comedian Chloe Petts join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
